title: citrus × limonia volcameriana
intro: Citrus × limonia volcameriana, also known as Calamondin, is a small evergreen tree or shrub in the family Rutaceae, and is a hybrid of the sour orange (Citrus × aurantium) and the lemon (Citrus limon). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial and antifungal properties, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea. The health benefits of Citrus × limonia volcameriana include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory issues, and reduce the risk of certain cancers due to its high content of vitamin C and other antioxidants. Citrus × limonia volcameriana can be prepared as an herbal tea, juice, or oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, indigestion, and skin conditions.
